entries.definition:
ع/م/م ʿ–m–m turban; encompassing, general, common; to be strong,
to become chief; the common people; uncle, paternal aunt. Of this
root, three forms occur five times in the Qur’an: عَمّ ʿamm once;
أَعْمَام ʾaʿmām once and عَمَّات ʿammāt three times.

عَمّ ʿamm [n. pl. أَعْمَام ʾaʿmām] paternal uncle (33:50) وَبَنَاتِ عَمِّكَ
and the daughters of your paternal uncle.

عَمَّات ʿammāt [pl. of n. عَمَّة ʿammatun] paternal aunt (4:23) حُرِّمَتْ
عَلَيْكُمْ أُمَّهَاتُكُمْ وَبَنَاتُكُمْ وَأَخَوَاتُكُمْ وَعَمَّاتُكُمْ ... forbidden to you [as wives] are
your mothers, daughters, sisters, paternal aunts ...
